activities.name: Select Tables That Require Masking
activities.description: Select tables with a data mapping and assigned algorithms.

sqlReaderQuery.value:
WITH
tables_requiring_masking AS
(
    SELECT DISTINCT
        dataset, specified_database, specified_schema, identified_table
    FROM @{pipeline().parameters.P_METADATA_SCHEMA}.@{pipeline().parameters.P_METADATA_RULESET_TABLE}
    WHERE assigned_algorithm IS NOT NULL
    AND assigned_algorithm != ''
    AND dataset = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_DATASET}'
    AND specified_database = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SOURCE_CONTAINER}'
    AND specified_schema LIKE '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SOURCE_DIRECTORY}%'
)
SELECT DISTINCT
	dm.source_dataset,
	dm.source_database,
	dm.source_schema,
    REVERSE(SUBSTRING(REVERSE(dm.source_schema), CHARINDEX('/', REVERSE(dm.source_schema)), LEN(dm.source_schema))) AS source_directory,
    REVERSE(SUBSTRING(REVERSE(dm.source_schema),0, CHARINDEX('/', REVERSE(dm.source_schema)))) AS source_prefix,
	dm.source_table,
	dm.sink_dataset,
	dm.sink_database,
	dm.sink_schema,
    REVERSE(SUBSTRING(REVERSE(dm.sink_schema), CHARINDEX('/', REVERSE(dm.sink_schema)), LEN(dm.sink_schema))) AS sink_directory,
    REVERSE(SUBSTRING(REVERSE(dm.source_schema),0, CHARINDEX('/', REVERSE(dm.source_schema)))) AS sink_prefix,
	dm.sink_table
FROM
	tables_requiring_masking rs
	JOIN @{pipeline().parameters.P_METADATA_SCHEMA}.@{pipeline().parameters.P_METADATA_SOURCE_TO_SINK_MAPPING_TABLE} dm
	ON (
		rs.dataset = dm.source_dataset AND
		rs.specified_database = dm.source_database AND
		rs.specified_schema = dm.source_schema AND
		rs.identified_table = dm.source_table
	)
WHERE
	dm.sink_database = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SINK_CONTAINER}'
    AND dm.sink_schema LIKE '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SINK_DIRECTORY}%'
	AND (dm.mapping_complete IS NULL OR dm.mapping_complete = 0)
	AND (ISJSON(dm.masked_status) = 0 OR dm.masked_status IS NULL)
UNION
SELECT DISTINCT
	dm.source_dataset,
	dm.source_database,
	dm.source_schema,
    REVERSE(SUBSTRING(REVERSE(dm.source_schema), CHARINDEX('/', REVERSE(dm.source_schema)), LEN(dm.source_schema))) AS source_directory,
    REVERSE(SUBSTRING(REVERSE(dm.source_schema),0, CHARINDEX('/', REVERSE(dm.source_schema)))) AS source_prefix,
	dm.source_table,
	dm.sink_dataset,
	dm.sink_database,
	dm.sink_schema,
    REVERSE(SUBSTRING(REVERSE(dm.sink_schema), CHARINDEX('/', REVERSE(dm.sink_schema)), LEN(dm.sink_schema))) AS sink_directory,
    REVERSE(SUBSTRING(REVERSE(dm.source_schema),0, CHARINDEX('/', REVERSE(dm.source_schema)))) AS sink_prefix,
	dm.sink_table
FROM
	tables_requiring_masking rs
	JOIN @{pipeline().parameters.P_METADATA_SCHEMA}.@{pipeline().parameters.P_METADATA_SOURCE_TO_SINK_MAPPING_TABLE} dm
	ON (
		rs.dataset = dm.source_dataset AND
		rs.specified_database = dm.source_database AND
		rs.specified_schema = dm.source_schema AND
		rs.identified_table = dm.source_table
	)
WHERE
	dm.sink_database = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SINK_CONTAINER}'
    AND dm.sink_schema LIKE '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SINK_DIRECTORY}%'
	AND (dm.mapping_complete IS NULL OR dm.mapping_complete = 0)
	AND ISJSON(dm.masked_status) = 1
	AND JSON_VALUE(dm.masked_status, '$') = 'false'

dependsOn.activity: Check If At Least One Column Is Masked

sqlReaderQuery.value:
SELECT TOP 1 source_metadata
FROM @{pipeline().parameters.P_METADATA_SCHEMA}.@{pipeline().parameters.P_METADATA_RULESET_TABLE}
WHERE dataset = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_DATASET}'
AND specified_database = '@{item().source_database}'
AND specified_schema = '@{item().source_schema}'
AND identified_table = '@{item().source_table}'

activities.name: Select Tables Without Required Masking
activities.description: Select tables with a data mapping, and no assigned algorithms.

dependsOn.activity: Check If At Least One Column Is Masked

sqlReaderQuery.value:
SELECT DISTINCT
    source_database,
    source_schema,
    REVERSE(SUBSTRING(REVERSE(source_schema), CHARINDEX('/', REVERSE(source_schema)), LEN(source_schema))) AS source_directory,
    REVERSE(SUBSTRING(REVERSE(source_schema),0, CHARINDEX('/', REVERSE(source_schema)))) AS source_prefix,
    source_table,
    sink_database,
    sink_schema,
    REVERSE(SUBSTRING(REVERSE(sink_schema), CHARINDEX('/', REVERSE(sink_schema)), LEN(sink_schema))) AS sink_directory,
    REVERSE(SUBSTRING(REVERSE(source_schema),0, CHARINDEX('/', REVERSE(source_schema)))) AS sink_prefix,
    sink_table
FROM @{pipeline().parameters.P_METADATA_SCHEMA}.@{pipeline().parameters.P_METADATA_RULESET_TABLE} rs
JOIN @{pipeline().parameters.P_METADATA_SCHEMA}.@{pipeline().parameters.P_METADATA_SOURCE_TO_SINK_MAPPING_TABLE} am
ON (am.source_database = rs.specified_database AND am.source_schema = rs.specified_schema AND am.source_table = rs.identified_table)
WHERE
    rs.dataset = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_DATASET}'
    AND am.source_dataset = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_DATASET}'
    AND am.sink_dataset = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_DATASET}'
    AND am.source_database = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SOURCE_CONTAINER}'
    AND am.sink_database = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SINK_CONTAINER}'
    AND am.source_schema LIKE '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SOURCE_DIRECTORY}%'
    AND am.sink_schema LIKE '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SINK_DIRECTORY}%'
EXCEPT
SELECT DISTINCT
    source_database,
    source_schema,
    REVERSE(SUBSTRING(REVERSE(source_schema), CHARINDEX('/', REVERSE(source_schema)), LEN(source_schema))) AS source_directory,
    REVERSE(SUBSTRING(REVERSE(source_schema),0, CHARINDEX('/', REVERSE(source_schema)))) AS source_prefix,
    source_table,
    sink_database,
    sink_schema,
    REVERSE(SUBSTRING(REVERSE(sink_schema), CHARINDEX('/', REVERSE(sink_schema)), LEN(sink_schema))) AS sink_directory,
    REVERSE(SUBSTRING(REVERSE(source_schema),0, CHARINDEX('/', REVERSE(source_schema)))) AS sink_prefix,
    sink_table
FROM @{pipeline().parameters.P_METADATA_SCHEMA}.@{pipeline().parameters.P_METADATA_RULESET_TABLE} rs
JOIN @{pipeline().parameters.P_METADATA_SCHEMA}.@{pipeline().parameters.P_METADATA_SOURCE_TO_SINK_MAPPING_TABLE} am
ON (am.source_database = rs.specified_database AND am.source_schema = rs.specified_schema AND am.source_table = rs.identified_table)
WHERE
    rs.dataset = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_DATASET}'
    AND rs.assigned_algorithm IS NOT NULL
    AND rs.assigned_algorithm != ''
    AND am.source_dataset = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_DATASET}'
    AND am.sink_dataset = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_DATASET}'
    AND am.source_database = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SOURCE_CONTAINER}'
    AND am.sink_database = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SINK_CONTAINER}'
    AND am.source_schema LIKE '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SOURCE_DIRECTORY}%'
    AND am.sink_schema LIKE '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SINK_DIRECTORY}%';

sqlReaderQuery.value:
SELECT TOP 1 source_metadata
FROM @{pipeline().parameters.P_METADATA_SCHEMA}.@{pipeline().parameters.P_METADATA_RULESET_TABLE}
WHERE dataset = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_DATASET}'
AND specified_database = '@{item().source_database}'
AND specified_schema = '@{item().source_schema}'
AND identified_table = '@{item().source_table}'

activities.name: Check If We Should Reapply Mapping

ifTrueActivities.name: Mark Table Mapping Incomplete
ifTrueActivities.description: Mark all tables in this database and schema as unmapped so that we can re-apply the mapping to them

text.value:
UPDATE
    @{pipeline().parameters.P_METADATA_SCHEMA}.@{pipeline().parameters.P_METADATA_SOURCE_TO_SINK_MAPPING_TABLE}
SET
    mapping_complete = CAST(0 AS BIT),
    masked_status = NULL
WHERE
    source_dataset = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_DATASET}'
    AND source_database = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SOURCE_CONTAINER}'
    AND source_schema LIKE '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SOURCE_DIRECTORY}%'
    AND sink_dataset = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_DATASET}'
    AND sink_database = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SINK_CONTAINER}'
    AND sink_schema LIKE '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SINK_DIRECTORY}%'

dependsOn.activity: Check If We Should Reapply Mapping

sqlReaderQuery.value:
SELECT DISTINCT
    dm.sink_dataset,
    dm.sink_database,
    dm.sink_schema,
    REVERSE(SUBSTRING(REVERSE(dm.sink_schema), CHARINDEX('/', REVERSE(dm.sink_schema)), LEN(dm.sink_schema))) AS sink_directory
FROM
    @{pipeline().parameters.P_METADATA_SCHEMA}.@{pipeline().parameters.P_METADATA_SOURCE_TO_SINK_MAPPING_TABLE} dm
WHERE
    dm.source_dataset = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_DATASET}'
    AND dm.source_database = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SOURCE_CONTAINER}'
    AND dm.source_schema LIKE '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SOURCE_DIRECTORY}%'
    AND dm.sink_dataset = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_DATASET}'
    AND dm.sink_database = '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SINK_CONTAINER}'
    AND dm.sink_schema LIKE '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SINK_DIRECTORY}%'
    AND (dm.mapping_complete IS NULL OR dm.mapping_complete = 0)
    AND LOWER('@{pipeline().parameters.P_TRUNCATE_SINK_BEFORE_WRITE}') = 'true'

activities.name: Check If At Least One Column Is Masked

message.value: No columns are masked in the Cassandra table '@{pipeline().parameters.P_SOURCE_CONTAINER}'. Please assign a masking algorithm to at least one column and try again.

sources.name: Source
sources.description: Select source data in source container DF_SOURCE_CONTAINER, and wildcard that is constructed based on DF_SOURCE_DIRECTORY, DF_SOURCE_PREFIX, and DF_SOURCE_TABLE using an inline dataset, with DF_COLUMN_DELIMITER, DF_QUOTE_CHARACTER, DF_ESCAPE_CHARACTER, and DF_NULL_VALUE as specified in the parameters, and storing the file name in DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_FILE_NAME

sinks.name: Sink
sinks.description: Sink results of masking to data store by sinking all columns but DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_BATCH_ID, DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_SORT_ID, and DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_FILE_NAME to the data sink, naming the file as DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_SINK_FILE_NAME, and using the same metadata settings as were used in the source with DF_COLUMN_DELIMITER, DF_QUOTE_CHARACTER, DF_ESCAPE_CHARACTER, and DF_NULL_VALUE

transformations.name: AddSortKey
transformations.description: Create column DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_SORT_ID that consists of SHA of the data across all columns in the table - every row will have this value and it cannot be null

transformations.name: SortBySortKey
transformations.description: Sort the table by the value in DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_SORT_ID, as we need the table to be in a particular order before we apply a surrogate key

transformations.name: CreateSurrogateKey
transformations.description: Add a DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_BATCH_ID column that increments by 1 and starts at 1 after applying the sorting

transformations.name: WrapValuesInArray
transformations.description: For each column we wish to mask, convert the value into an array, this is needed to preserve null values as null when using collect, as null values become [], and encode the column names to handle body type mapping changes

transformations.name: SelectColumnsUnmasked
transformations.description: Select only columns that don't require masking

transformations.name: AggregateColumnsByBatch
transformations.description: For each column we wish to mask, aggregate to a list using collect, grouped by DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_BATCH_ID batches by using DF_TARGET_BATCH_SIZE and the number of columns being masked, name the group as DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_BATCH_GROUP

transformations.name: FlattenValuesOutOfArray
transformations.description: For each column we wish to mask, flatten the value out of the array, in the case where the value was previously [], it becomes null

transformations.name: AssertNoFailures
transformations.description: Confirm that we received a 200 response status from the API request

transformations.name: FlattenAggregateData
transformations.description: Unroll the API response body into decoded named columns

transformations.name: JoinMaskedAndUnmaskedData
transformations.description: Inner join on SelectColumnsUnmasked and FlattenAggregateData based on matching DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_BATCH_ID

transformations.name: CreateSinkFileName
transformations.description: Create column DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_SINK_FILE_NAME by replacing DF_SOURCE_DIRECTORY with DF_SINK_DIRECTORY in the value in DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_FILE_NAME

transformations.name: CreateAlterRow
transformations.description: Add an alter row condition so that all rows will be inserted into the existing table with the correct column order

transformations.name: CombineRows
transformations.description: Union the output of RemoveAmbiguousColumn with the empty CreateAlterRow table, and performing the union by name

transformations.name: RemoveAmbiguousColumn
transformations.description: Since both FlattenAggregateData and SelectColumnsUnmasked have a column DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_BATCH_ID, we need to remove that column, so select all columns whose name isn't DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_BATCH_ID

transformations.name: RemoveAllData
transformations.description: To preserve the order of the columns, we will remove all rows from the table by filtering on false()

scriptLines: DF_BODY_TYPE_MAPPING as string ('(timestamp as date, status as string, message as string, trace_id as string, items as (DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_BATCH_ID as long)[])'),
body as (timestamp as date, status as string, message as string, trace_id as string, items as (DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_BATCH_ID as long)[]),

DCSForAzureAPI assert(expectTrue(toInteger(regexExtract(status, '(\\d+)', 1)) == 200, false, 'Failed_request', null, iif(isNull(body.message), status, concatWS(', ', 'timestamp: ' + toString(body.timestamp), 'status: ' + body.status, 'message: ' + body.message, 'trace_id: ' + body.trace_id))),

sources.name: Source
sources.description: Select source data in source container DF_SOURCE_CONTAINER, and wildcard that is constructed based on DF_SOURCE_DIRECTORY, DF_SOURCE_PREFIX, and DF_SOURCE_TABLE using an inline dataset, with DF_COLUMN_DELIMITER, DF_QUOTE_CHARACTER, DF_ESCAPE_CHARACTER, and DF_NULL_VALUE as specified in the parameters, and storing the file name in DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_FILE_NAME

sinks.name: Sink
sinks.description: Sink results of masking to data store by sinking all columns but DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_BATCH_ID, DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_SORT_ID, and DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_FILE_NAME to the data sink, naming the file as DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_SINK_FILE_NAME, and using the same metadata settings as were used in the source with DF_COLUMN_DELIMITER, DF_QUOTE_CHARACTER, DF_ESCAPE_CHARACTER, and DF_NULL_VALUE

transformations.name: CreateSinkFileName
transformations.description: Create column DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_SINK_FILE_NAME by replacing DF_SOURCE_DIRECTORY with DF_SINK_DIRECTORY in the value in DELPHIX_COMPLIANCE_SERVICE_FILE_NAME
